narrative: Fictiv is a digital manufacturing marketplace ('AWS of manufacturing'): product companies upload proprietary CAD/drawings under NDA, and Fictiv routes them to a vetted global network of contract manufacturers across China, India, Japan, and Mexico for quoting and production. The demo spine is that single data flow — customer crown-jewel IP plus EAR/ITAR export-controlled technical data transiting Fictiv and fanning out to offshore partners. We lead with the IP-to-offshore leak and the export-control marking (uniquely Fictiv), wrap in platform secrets / PCI / global supplier payouts / HR, and close on precision: manufacturing is full of part and drawing numbers shaped like PANs and SSNs that a good detector must stay quiet on.

findings.finding: Digital manufacturing platform — CAD-upload instant quoting + a managed global partner network delivering custom mechanical parts (CNC, injection molding, 3D printing, sheet metal, casting). 42M+ parts delivered; 6,000+ customers incl. ~61% of the Fortune 500.

Aerospace, medical devices, automotive, robotics, climate tech, semiconductor, eVTOL/EV; named publicly: Medtronic, Johnson & Johnson, RBC Bearings. Many stealth hardware startups with unreleased products.

Fictiv's Terms permit only EAR99 / ECCN 9E991 parts; no CCL/USML technical data may be uploaded. They scan technical drawings for export-control text and AUTO-DELETE any quote/files where 'ITAR' is detected, purging the data from their databases and servers.
Fictiv publishes an IP/confidentiality policy describing how customer designs and CAD data are protected under NDA across its partner network.
Manufacturing centers in US, China, India, Mexico; supply regions also include Japan. 4,100+ material/process/finish combinations across a vetted partner network.
Node.js, JavaScript/TypeScript, GraphQL; AWS services SQS, Lambda, EC2, RDS. SQL & NoSQL DBs, event buses/queues.
~400 employees; $190M+ raised; acquired by MISUMI Group (Japan) on Apr 17, 2025. Plans to grow headcount. ISO 9001:2015 certified.
Instant-quote-to-order platform charges customers for orders (card payment) and pays a large global supplier base, implying both PCI cardholder data and cross-border supplier banking/tax data.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Customer design IP (CAD/drawings/specs in prose & files) leaks to the wrong offshore partner or region via a sourcing handoff.
hypotheses.basis: Core data flow: NDA-bound customer IP transits the platform out to a global partner network (finding: business model + IP confidentiality).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Export-controlled technical data (ECCN/ITAR/USML markings) crosses borders to a China-based partner — a regulator-facing violation.
hypotheses.basis: Fictiv's own export-control policy: only EAR99/9E991 allowed, ITAR auto-deleted (finding: export control).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Manufacturing identifiers (part/drawing/quote numbers) shaped like PANs/SSNs cause false positives — the alert-fatigue/precision story.
hypotheses.basis: Their work product is full of structured numeric identifiers (inference from manufacturing domain).

hypotheses.hypothesis: A leaked platform secret (AWS/RDS/Stripe) exposes the system holding 6,000 customers' IP at once.
hypotheses.basis: Platform is the vault; stack confirmed Node/TS/GraphQL on AWS RDS/SQS/Lambda (finding: tech stack).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Global supplier payouts + a US/India/Mexico workforce spread foreign IBANs, India PAN, and SSNs across finance/HR surfaces.
hypotheses.basis: 4,100+ partners paid globally; ~400 staff across US/India/Mexico (findings: global footprint, scale).

decisions.choice: Lead the demo with customer-IP-to-offshore + export-control markings, not the generic secrets/PII layers.
decisions.rationale: These are uniquely Fictiv and existential to a marketplace built on NDA trust + EAR/ITAR compliance; a generic competitor's demo would miss them.
decisions.drove: rfq_supplier_handoff.eml and program_slack.txt as the lead carriers; design_ip and export_control_marking data types.

decisions.choice: Email, GitHub, Google Drive/warehouse, Slack, product/billing — 7 carriers.
decisions.rationale: Maps to Fictiv's actual collaboration + code + data stack and the supply-chain seam where IP moves.

decisions.choice: Supply Chain / Sourcing Manager as protagonist of the lead surfaces, threaded with a DFM engineer, platform engineer, AP, HR, plus a fictional customer and a Shenzhen partner.
decisions.rationale: The defining workforce sits at the customer-IP / external-supplier boundary — the leak Fictiv fears most.

decisions.choice: Part/drawing/quote numbers shaped like PANs + an SSN-shaped employee ID + placeholder keys + git SHA.
decisions.rationale: Work-product near-misses (manufacturing identifiers) are the most resonant precision story for this prospect.
decisions.drove: order_id_like_pan reused as part/drawing numbers; internal_id_like_ssn; placeholder_key; git_sha plants tagged fp_near_miss.

decisions.choice: Design IP in prose; export markings in body text; spaced PAN/SSN/IBAN; base64/line-split secrets.
decisions.rationale: Recall story grounded in how their data actually appears (free-text IP, international partners formatting numbers loosely).
decisions.drove: fn_evasive plants across the email, payouts, HR, order, and Slack carriers.

decisions.choice: Add india_pan -> INDIA_PAN_INDIVIDUAL to detectors.json data_type_map; leave design_ip and export_control_marking as not_integrated.
decisions.rationale: Shows Nightfall's international coverage for a global company, AND honestly surfaces the coverage gap on design-IP/export markings that motivates a custom detector — a real talking point, not a hidden failure.
decisions.drove: supplier_payouts India PAN plants detected; design_ip/export markings shown as not_integrated in the report card.

decisions.choice: Full POC — 7 carriers, ~30 planted values, even FP/FN mix.
decisions.rationale: User confirmed full POC for a real demo/bake-off walkthrough.

open_questions: Does Fictiv use Slack or Microsoft Teams for internal/partner coordination? Assumed Slack; swap surface if Teams.
Post-MISUMI integration may add Japan-based data flows / MISUMI systems as a surface — deferred; could add a MISUMI integration email carrier later.
Real supplier coordination may happen over WeChat/email in-region rather than Slack; modeled the offshore handoff as email which is the safer assumption.
